rulings.Case Title: MELINDA BARANY et al VS. MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS MELINDA BARANY and ALBERT KUN'S Motion to Compel Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to May 24, 2013 at 9:00 am pursuant to the plaintiffs' request in order to allow plaintiff time to review the records recently submitted by the defendant. Further briefing is required to narrow the issues in light of the recently propounded records. Plaintiff shall file a supplemental brief addressing what issues remain and what issues are withdrawn no later than May 13, 2013 at 4 pm. Defendant may file a responsive brief no later May 17, 2013 at 4 pm. The supplemental briefs shall be no more than 7 pages long and shall be served by email, personal delivery or fax. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 at the time of filing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COMPWEST INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MANUEL PEDROSA CONTRERAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File Amendment To Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF COMPWEST INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Leave To File Amendment To Complaint. Motion for leave to file amendment to Complaint to add a new party is granted pursuant to CCP 473(a) in the furtherance of justice. The trial date will be continued until October 30, 2013 to allow Defendant Contreras to file a motion for summary judgment/adjudication. The motion is denied as to CCP 474; moving party did not show that it was genuinely ignorant of the potential liability and identity of Tropicana & Russell Inc., dba Marina Food after moving party's agent (TransPac Solutions) exchanged correspondence with Argonaut Great Central Insurance Company on October 26, 2009 and November 10, 2009 (Exhibit A to Declaration of Sean Moriarty filed in opposition to motion.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: COMPWEST INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MANUEL PEDROSA CONTRERAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Of Amendment To Complt In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 13, INTERVENOR DAVID CANIZALES Motion To File Amendment To Complaint In Intervention. Motion for leave to file amendment to Complaint in Intervention pursuant to CCP 474 is denied. Moving party did not show that it was genuinely ignorant of the potential liability and identity of Tropicana & Russell Inc., dba Marina Foods ("T&R") in light of the fact that Mr. Canizales settled with T&R, signed a release of all claims dated June 7, 2011, and cashed a check for $5000 that listed T&R as the insured. The Court will construe the motion to also request relief pursuant to CCP 473, and in the furtherance of justice will allow the filing of an amendment to the complaint in intervention to add T&R as a new party. The trial date will be continued until October 30, 2013 to allow Defendant Contreras to file a motion for summary judgment/adjudication. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL K. MILLER VS. LUXOR CAB COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss (Terminating Sanctions) Based On Pltf'S Failure To Comply With This Court'S Order Compelling Pltf To Respond To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 3. DEFENDANT LUXOR CABS, INC. (erroneously sued herein as LUXOR CAB COMPANY)'s Motion to Dismiss (Terminating Sanctions) Based on Plaintiff's Failure to Comply with this Court's Order Compelling Plaintiff to Respond to Discovery Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties are ordered to appear. At the hearing the court will ask the defendant to verify that the pro per plaintiff's address is 125 Cambon Drive Apt MD, San Francisco, California 94132. If at the time of the hearing the plaintiff has not yet provided the defendant with complete and comprehensive verified responses to the special interrogatories and request for production of documents, the motion will be granted and the case will be dismissed with prejudice. If the discovery is provided by the time of the hearing, (in a form and substance acceptable to the court), the motion will be denied but plaintiff will be ordered to pay defendants costs and attorney fees incurred in bringing this motion, based on offers of proof at the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL K. MILLER VS. LUXOR CAB COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Deem Matters Admitted Based On Pltf'S Failure To Respond To Requests For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 2. DEFENDANT LUXOR CABS, INC. (erroneously sued herein as LUXOR CAB COMPANY)'s Motion to Deem Matters Admitted Based on Plaintiff's Failure to Respond to Requests for Admissions Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The parties are ordered to appear. At the hearing the court will ask the defendant to verify that the pro per plaintiff's address is 125 Cambon Drive Apt MD, San Francisco, California 94132. If at the time of the hearing the plaintiff has not yet provided the defendant with complete and comprehensive verified responses to the requests for admissions, the motion will be granted and the requests will be deemed admitted. If the responses are provided by the time of the hearing, (in a form and substance acceptable to the court), the motion will be denied but plaintiff will be ordered to pay defendants costs and attorney fees incurred in bringing this motion, based on offers of proof at the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 700 ILLINOIS STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. BESTCO LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT TIPCO CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. Regarding the first cause of action, Plaintiff has pled that that Civil Code section 895 et seq. applies. This is sufficient. Defendants should proceed with discovery. Regarding the second and third causes of action, the Court cannot determine from the face of the complaint that these causes of action are barred, and Plaintiff is entitled to plead in the alternative. The eighth cause of action is sufficiently pled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TARA GANANCE VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT STUART MANSFIELD, M.D.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend in its entirety. Plaintiffs cannot recover general damages or loss of earnings damages. Turpin v. Sortini (1982) 31 Cal.3d 220, 237; Andalon v. Sup. Crt. (1984) 162 Cal.App.3d 600, 614. Plaintiff fails to highlight a basis for attorney's fees. The prayer for attorney's fees is improper because plaintiff failed to comply with CCP 425.13. The Court read and considered Plaintiff's Opposition to this motion, which was filed on April 22, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KIM FERGUSON VS. JACQUELINE A. DELORD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defts Subpoenas For Employment, Insurance, Medicla And Psychiatric Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 1, 2013, Line 4. PLAINTIFF KIM FERGUSON'S Motion to Quash Defendants Subpoenas for Employment, Insurance, Medical, and Psychiatric Records Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is granted in part and denied in part. The employment records will not be produced. The remaining subpoenas will be allowed subject to the proposed limiting language set forth at pages 5-7 of defendant's opposition. All records will be produced subject to a confidentiality agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
